params.name: Coral Fossil
params.description: The coral is a marine multicellular invertebrate that could secrete calcium carbonate and form a hard skeleton in its body. With a tough body, it was often mistaken for a rock. As its body comprises many small individual corals, the surface of the stone-like outer shell was filled with holes of different sizes. Those with trypophobia, you've been warned.
